news.title: Ambuja Cements' profit surges over four-fold to ₹2,302 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Ambuja Cements' profit after tax surged over four times in Q2 FY26compared to the previous year. This growth was driven by strong revenue increases in both cement and ready-mix concrete businesses. Ambuja Cements also achieved its highest ever sales volume for the second quarter.

news.title: Smartworks leases over 8.15 lakh sq ft workspace at Eastbridge, Mumbai
news.para: Smartworks Coworking Spaces has secured a significant lease of over 8.15 lakh sq ft at Regalia Office Parks' Eastbridge development in Vikhroli West, Mumbai. This new center is slated to open in Q4 2026. This expansion follows a recent 5.57 lakh sq ft lease at Tata Realty's Intellion Park in Navi Mumbai.

news.title: Shriram Properties jointly develop five-acre land in South Bengaluru
news.para: Shriram Properties is set to develop a premium villa community on five acres in Bengaluru's Bannerghatta Road. This prime location offers excellent connectivity and amenities, making it attractive to homebuyers. The project, with an estimated gross development value exceeding ₹350 crore, is slated for launch in fiscal year 2026, promising exclusivity and convenience.

news.title: Land lease defaulters owe Kerala government ₹665 crore
news.para: The state has identified 584 individuals and organizations occupying government-leased land without paying dues or violating lease terms, accumulating arrears of Rs 665.30 crore. Despite significant financial dues, only a negligible amount of land has been reclaimed, and the government lacks a compiled record of total illegally occupied land.

news.title: Bhubaneswar: 100 more flats handed over in third phase at Shantipalli Awas Yojana
news.para: Bhubaneswar Municipal Corporation and Bhubaneswar Development Authority have allotted over 100 more units at Shantipalli Awas Yojana, bringing the total beneficiaries to 560. This marks the third phase of allotment for Keshari Residency, one of three affordable housing projects under PM Awas Yojana in the city. The project, a public-private partnership, will eventually comprise 1,300 dwelling units.

news.title: HC directs Haryana government to explain delay in constituting SEIAA after February
news.para: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has ordered the State of Haryana to provide a specific affidavit. This affidavit must clarify why the State Environment Impact Assessment Authority has not been constituted after February 2025. The court also seeks all correspondence between the state and the Union government on this matter.

news.title: Real estate companies from other cities flock to NCR on rising demand, prices
news.para: In a surprising turn of events, developers from Mumbai and Bengaluru are setting their sights on the National Capital Region, where property prices are outpacing growth seen elsewhere. Real estate giants are not just launching new projects; they are also significantly enlarging their market footprint.

news.title: Register Lucknow development body's properties at special camp from Nov 3-15
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is organizing a 13-day 'single-window' camp from November 3-15 to expedite pending property registrations.  LDA officials will prepare documents for the first 10 days, with the registry department finalizing registrations on-site during the last three days. Allottees who have paid in full or 80% or more are being contacted to complete their property registries.

news.title: PNB Housing Finance shortlists four for MD & CEO position
news.para: PNB Housing Finance is close to appointing a new Managing Director and CEO. Four candidates have been shortlisted, with Ajai Shukla of Tata Capital Housing Finance leading the race. The company has submitted the names to regulators for approval. This appointment follows multiple leadership changes in recent years. An update on the selection process is expected soon.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh clears map for township near Hindon terminal
news.para: After a 28-year delay, the UP Housing and Development Board has finally approved the Ajantapuram township layout in Sikandarpur. This significant clearance, overcoming land compensation disputes and legal battles with cooperative societies, paves the way for development. The 274-hectare project, near Hindon Civil Terminal, will offer housing for 4,000 people and is expected to take five years to complete.

news.title: New World Development launches up to $1.9 billion debt exchange offer
news.para: New World Development is taking bold steps to safeguard its financial foundation as it restructures its debt obligations. The Hong Kong firm has announced a notable $1.9 billion bid for its perpetual securities, strategically designed to enhance liquidity and extend the maturity of its debts. Confronted with a tough and declining property market, the company previously postponed coupon payments.

news.title: Nagpur civic body cracks down on property tax defaulters with tech-driven warrant system
news.para: Nagpur Municipal Corporation is intensifying property tax recovery, targeting chronic defaulters with a new automated warrant alert system.  Leveraging India Post's Proof of Delivery, the NMC ensures invoices reach owners.  Those ignoring confirmed deliveries now face immediate SMS alerts for warrant execution, signaling a shift to strict enforcement to recover over Rs1,000 crore in arrears.

news.title: Mangaluru: ₹135 crore tender floated for tech park under PPP model
news.para: Mangaluru, the 'Silicon Beach of India', is set to get its first purpose-built tech park. Karnataka State Electronics Development Corporation (KEONICS) has floated a Rs 135 crore tender for the project, which will be developed under a public-private partnership (PPP) model.

news.title: NGT directs MoEFCC to review environmental clearance for Gurugram housing project
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has stepped in, instructing the Union Environment Ministry to reassess the environmental clearance for a proposed housing project expansion in Gurugram. This decision arises from challenges brought forth by a resident welfare group.

news.title: No 40% circle rate hike now, but Ghaziabad tweaks shop rates
news.para: Ghaziabad administration has shelved a proposed 40% property circle rate hike, opting for minor technical corrections instead. The decision comes after a drop in property registrations, with officials aiming to avoid further revenue decline. Existing rates largely remain from 2024, with slight increases for small commercial properties.

news.title: Hyderabad: HYDRAA razes five-storey illegal building in Miyapur
news.para: HYDRAA demolished a five-storey illegal building in Miyapur that encroached upon 473 square yards of HMDA land. Developers from Bhanu Constructions allegedly used fabricated LRS documents and a forged demand draft to conceal the encroachment. Police have registered an FIR following the demolition.

news.title: Builders in Karnataka face massive fines for plan violations
news.para: As per the new rates, residential, industrial, and other non-commercial structures in Town Panchayat areas will pay ₹1,000 per sq metre, and commercial buildings will be fined ₹1,500 per sq metre.

news.title: Karnataka RERA to verify projects under new closure policy
news.para: Karnataka Rera is set to implement a project closure policy, mirroring Odisha's initiative to enhance transparency and accountability in real estate. This policy will ensure builders meet all statutory and service compliances before projects are officially closed, safeguarding homebuyers from fraud and delays.

news.title: Bhubaneswar: BDA upholds order directing defaulting homeowners to pay maintenance fees
news.para: Bhubaneswar Development Authority has ordered defaulting residents of a housing project to pay over Rs 2 lakh in maintenance fees within a month. The authority upheld its earlier decision, empowering the allottees' association to recover dues. This ruling follows a dispute where some occupants refused payment, claiming the project wasn't fully handed over.
